edges.description: Reward hypersensitivity is represented upstream of recurrent binge-eating episodes.
Reduced inhibitory control is represented upstream of recurrent binge-eating episodes.

nodes.description: Recurrent binge eating is the defining clinical phenotype of binge eating disorder. The HPO term is broad, so the preferred term records the more specific BED manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy and Lisdexamfetamine, Alone and Combined, for Binge-Eating Disorder With Obesity: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
evidence.snippet: Intention-to-treat binge-eating remission rates differed significantly between treatments, with CBT+LDX having the highest remission rate (70.2%) followed by CBT (44.7%) and LDX (40.4%).

nodes.description: Impaired inhibitory control is represented as a separate top-down control mechanism that can fail to suppress binge-eating behavior.

evidence.reference_title: Reward and Inhibitory Control as Mechanisms and Treatment Targets for Binge Eating Disorder
evidence.snippet: Reward hypersensitivity and impaired inhibitory control are mechanisms underlying binge eating disorder.

nodes.description: Binge eating disorder is modeled as altered reward processing, including increased food reward and anticipatory reward responses that maintain binge-eating behavior.

biological_processes: G protein-coupled dopamine receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Reward and Inhibitory Control as Mechanisms and Treatment Targets for Binge Eating Disorder
evidence.snippet: Reward hypersensitivity and impaired inhibitory control are mechanisms underlying binge eating disorder.
